Summary: This study explores the potential of FET PET radiomics for differentiating pseudoprogression from early tumor progression in glioma patients. The radiomics model, based on static FET PET scans, achieved 100% sensitivity and negative predictive value in the test cohort, suggesting its potential as a diagnostic tool. The model uses only four parameters, enhancing interpretability and clinical acceptance. Further validation in a large multicentric dataset and combination with MRI radiomics are suggested.

Item1.reason: The study does not explicitly mention adherence to any specific radiomics or machine learning checklists or guidelines.

Item2.reason: The study clearly defines eligibility criteria for patient inclusion, specifying the type of glioma, confirmation of diagnosis according to WHO classification, and MRI changes suspicious for tumor progression within 12 weeks after chemoradiation.

Item3.reason: The reference standard is based on pathology after repeated tumor resection or biopsy, or clinicoradiological follow-up assessed every 8–12 weeks according to the criteria described by Young and colleagues [52]. In the cases without neuropathological confirmation, pseudoprogression was diagnosed if (i) the imaging findings regressed or remained stable during follow-up MRI, (ii) no change in treatment was required for at least six months after completion of chemoradiation with concurrent temozolomide, and (iii) the patient was clinically stable. Early tumor progression was diagnosed if MRI changes were associated with clinical deterioration and prompted a change in treatment. This is a reasonable reference standard.

Item4.reason: The study involves data from a single institution.

Item5.reason: FET PET imaging is increasingly used in clinical practice for brain tumor imaging, and the study uses established protocols for image acquisition and analysis.

Item6.reason: The imaging protocol is described in detail, including the injected dose, scan duration, scanner model, reconstruction parameters, and voxel size.

Item7.reason: The study mentions that all patients underwent dynamic FET PET within 7–10 days after the first MRI within 12 weeks after completion of chemoradiation with temozolomide. This time interval is appropriate to validate the presence or absence of target conditions of the radiomics analysis at the moment of the diagnostic imaging exams.

Condition2.reason: The segmentation process involves a three-dimensional auto-contouring process using a tumor-to-brain ratio (TBR) of 1.6 or more, which is likely automated but does not exclude potential manual adjustments or review.
